title: ADHD May Not Be a Focus Problem. It May Be a Wakefulness Problem.
description: A 2026 study found that ADHD brains produce measurable sleep-like neural oscillations during waking hours — a discovery that reframes one of the most diagnosed conditions in medicine. If attention deficit is really a disorder of consciousness-state regulation, it changes not just how we understand ADHD, but why stimulants work at all.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for ADHD May Not Be a Focus Problem. It May Be a Wakefulness Problem.
